plant_profile.history: Navy Beans is a staple food crop cultivated globally, with profound references in traditional diets and Ayurvedic Ahara (dietary) regimens.
plant_profile.physical_characteristics: Typical morphological features of the legume family. Cultivated widely for its nutritional seeds/grains.
plant_profile.distribution: Cultivated globally in agricultural zones suitable for its growth.

medicinal_uses.muscle_building: High protein content in Navy Beans supports tissue building and muscle repair.
medicinal_uses.digestive_health: Rich in dietary fiber, promoting regular bowel movements and gut health.
medicinal_uses.metabolic_regulation: Low glycemic index helps in managing blood sugar levels.

standardization.api_reference: Nutritional standards and food safety guidelines applicable to Navy Beans.

preparation_methods.soaking: Soak overnight to reduce phytates and improve digestibility.
preparation_methods.cooking: Boil thoroughly until soft. Often tempered (Tadka) with cumin, asafoetida, and ghee to reduce Vata aggravation.

dosage.elderly: Cooked thoroughly, preferably as a thin soup.

contraindications: Limit intake if prone to severe Vata disorders (bloating, gas) unless cooked with digestive spices.
side_effects: Can cause flatulence if not soaked and cooked properly.
drug_interactions: No significant interactions when consumed as food.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Generally recognized as safe for food consumption.
